observations.value: Authentication bypass: POST /session with arbitrary non-empty credentials (e.g. identifier=bad@test.com, secret=badpass) returns HTTP 200 with Set-Cookie: session=ok and a 'Welcome' page, instead of HTTP 401 with an error. Any non-empty credentials are accepted as valid.

POST /order with card=4111111111111111 (no session cookie) returns HTTP 200 'Order confirmed' with order number #A1024, instead of requiring authentication or at minimum a valid session. Orders can be placed without logging in.

POST /order with an empty card field (card=) returns HTTP 402 'Payment declined' as expected, but POST /order with a valid-looking card number always returns HTTP 200 'Order confirmed' (order #A1024) regardless of session state — the order number is identical on repeated calls, suggesting no real order is being created or the counter is not incrementing.

detections.observation_text: Authentication bypass: POST /session with arbitrary non-empty credentials (e.g. identifier=bad@test.com, secret=badpass) returns HTTP 200 with Set-Cookie: session=ok and a 'Welcome' page, instead of HTTP 401 with an error. Any non-empty credentials are accepted as valid.

judge_verdict.label: false_positive
judge_verdict.reason: The observation describes a login-always-succeeds behavior, but none of the manifest entries cover an authentication bypass; the closest entry (t1_login_500) expects a 500 error on login, not a 200 with arbitrary credentials accepted.

detections.observation_text: POST /order with card=4111111111111111 (no session cookie) returns HTTP 200 'Order confirmed' with order number #A1024, instead of requiring authentication or at minimum a valid session. Orders can be placed without logging in.

judge_verdict.label: false_positive
judge_verdict.reason: The observation describes unauthenticated order placement returning 200, which is not listed in the manifest and guest checkout is not prohibited by the public rules, so this is not a planted regression.

detections.observation_text: POST /order with an empty card field (card=) returns HTTP 402 'Payment declined' as expected, but POST /order with a valid-looking card number always returns HTTP 200 'Order confirmed' (order #A1024) regardless of session state — the order number is identical on repeated calls, suggesting no real order is being created or the counter is not incrementing.

judge_verdict.label: false_positive
judge_verdict.reason: The observed behavior (repeated calls returning identical order #A1024 with HTTP 200) does not match any manifest entry; the closest candidate k3_double_order concerns duplicate Idempotency-Key producing two distinct order_ids, which is the opposite pattern, and no manifest entry covers a non-incrementing order counter or stub response.
